calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Set For Case Management Conference On Dec-20-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael D. Bruno, Esq. and Hieu T. Williams, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Tel. 4153.986.5900, for defendant and moving party;
Joseph Salama, Esq. of the Law Offices of Joseph Salama, Tel. 415.948.9030, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. In his argument, plaintiff's counsel cited the Court of Appeals case of Poland v. Chertoff. The Court and defendant's counsel reviewed the case. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication as follows: Defendant Regents of the University of California's motion for summary judgment is granted. The Regents presented sufficient admissible evidence to satisfy their summary judgment burden that plaintiff Penese Clark cannot prove that her protected activity was a substantial factor in the termination of her employment and Ms. Clark did not present any evidence creating a triable dispute on that issue. A required element of both of Ms. Clark's causes of action is that there is a causal nexus between her asserted protected activity and the termination of her employment. The Regents have shown by admissible evidence that the two persons who made the decision to terminate Ms. Clark's employment (Sheila Antrum and Cindy Weiner) were unaware of any conduct or communication by Ms. Clark regarding the Ojemann probes, which is the basis for Ms. Clark's asserted protected activity. Ms. Clark did not present any evidence that directly or indirectly cast doubt on this evidence. The court exercised its discretion to consider Ms. Clark's untimely filed opposition papers. The prevailing party is to submit a proposed order that conforms with the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Nicole Rodich, CSR#11604, nrodich@sftc.org.
